steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The student’s stated goal is to support the claim that the Hina Hanta archive features both older and newer artifacts. The correct choice must provide specific evidence of both time periods, not just one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The rubber stamp (*isht inchunwa*) was made in the 1800s.’: Mentions only one era (the 1800s). It provides zero evidence of newer artifacts, so it cannot support a claim about both old and new. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Hina Hanta, an online archive, features both basketry and household items.’: Distinguishes artifact types, not time periods. It says nothing about when any item was made, so it fails to address the old-versus-new claim. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Not all artifacts in the Hina Hanta archive are from the 1800s.’: Implies the existence of newer artifacts but provides no concrete example. A vague negation does not effectively support a specific claim. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The Hina Hanta online archive features cultural artifacts both old and new, including a rubber stamp from the 1800s and a cane trunk basket from 2019.’: Directly names one artifact from the 1800s and one from 2019, giving concrete evidence of both older and newer items. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
